page: Amazon Web Services m5n.24xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor with 48 physical cores and 96 virtual CPUs (vCPUs) running at 3.1 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results indicate a clear performance divergence between single-core and multi-core operations. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression fall into the bottom 25% tier. Conversely, multi-core capabilities are robust, with stress-ng multi-core performance, PassMark CPU score, and PassMark encryption tests scoring in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is also a strong point, with both small-block cached and large-block uncached read-only operations ranking in the top 25%.

The instance features a high baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps, making it applicable for network-intensive workloads. However, the lack of local NVMe storage and poor text generation performance on medium (7B) LLM models (bottom 10%) represent notable tradeoffs. Given the high memory bandwidth and strong multi-core performance, this server is applicable for database operations, multi-threaded data processing, and high-throughput network applications, while being less effective for single-threaded tasks or local-storage-dependent workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services m5n.24xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 96 vCPUs and 384.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks reveal a significant contrast: while single-core processing and single-threaded compression rank in the bottom 25%, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are highly competitive, placing in the top 25% of tested systems. This resource profile offers high compute density and memory throughput, though single-threaded efficiency is limited. The instance is applicable for network-heavy applications, multi-threaded data processing, and database operations that leverage its high network capacity and multi-core capabilities.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5n.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 96 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5n.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A high-bandwidth, multi-core general purpose instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 100 Gbps networking for data-intensive and network-heavy workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores
384.0 GB of DDR4 memory with strong read and write bandwidth
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput workloads
Strong multi-core CPU and encryption benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Applicable for database operations and multi-threaded network applications
